title: Windblown lead carbonate as the main source of lead in blood of children from a seaside community: an example of local birds as "canaries in the mine".
ehr_evidence: The study used a voluntary blood Pb (PbB) survey and environmental sampling; no EHR or linked health data were mentioned.
summary: Isotopic investigation of lead (Pb) exposure in children and adults in Esperance, Western Australia, following a mass die-off of native birds linked to lead carbonate concentrate from the Magellan mine.
key_findings: Isotope testing confirmed that lead in birds, soil, water, and sediment matched the lead from the Magellan mine.
The study reports isotopic investigation of blood in children and adults.
population: Children and adults in Esperance, Western Australia
